derived_by: opus reviewer E (adversarial, independent of all deriving batches)
notes: counts: 75 reviewed / 75 VERIFIED / 0 REFUTED / 0 STALE / ~20 unreviewed (of ~95 total edges across the 4 in-scope fragments). No edge flipped — the four fragments cite code/schema faithfully; caveats below are cite-quality notes, not refutations.

CRPIX finding (MANDATORY): NOT a bug — the two writers do NOT genuinely differ by one pixel. package.ts:159 writes CRPIX1: fitted.crpix[0] with no +1 because that block is the RECEIPT WCS intermediate, explicitly labeled engine 0-based at package.ts:170 (COMMENT: 'engine pixel convention (0-based, y-down)'). The +1 is applied downstream at the FITS serialization boundary: fits_writer.ts:209 writes fitsCard('CRPIX1', crpix1 + 1, 'FITS 1-based (engine 0-based +1)') where crpix1 = the receipt's 0-based CRPIX1. tools/stack/fits_io.mjs:218 writes wcs.crpix[0] + 1 directly because it consumes a raw ENGINE WCS (0-based) and emits FITS cards in one hop. So both FITS outputs are 1-based; they apply +1 at different chain positions (fits_writer after the receipt intermediate, stacker in one hop) — the documented 'two conversion boundaries, never both' de-dupe seam (stack/fits_io.mjs:195-208, fits_writer.ts:26-27). Consistent by design.

SUB-FINDING (schema self-citation drift, informational — does NOT flip any edge): binary_layouts.ts fits_io entry cites its own conversion sites with STALE line numbers — strideRule:301 says 'fits_decoder.ts:403' but hard.ra_hint = ra/15 is actually at fits_decoder.ts:1127; strideRule:302 + notes:316 say 'package.ts:77 + :101' but crval[0]*15 is actually at package.ts:161 (fitted) and :185 (synthesized). The review EDGES correctly cite the current lines (fits_decoder 1127, package 161) — the fragments effectively corrected the schema's stale internal refs. Worth a schema-refresh but out of scope for edge verdicts.

SCOPE: fully verified all 7 unit-conversion edges, all 9 loud_edges_c2 sub-stage solver/WCS edges, all 38 loud_edges_c boundary/stage edges except 1 (tauri, unread schema line), stage_semantic_a control+fallback (all 6) + 12 of its data-flow, and 9 evidence_b edges (byte-exact test assertions sampled). Unreviewed (22): stage_semantic_a data-flow at orchestrator lines I did not open (load->decode:641, decode->luminance:995, luminance->detect:1106, the 3 hardware_profile edges:1662/1831); the tauri boundary edge (binary_layouts:372); and 15 evidence_b edges whose cites are apispec/e2e test files I did not open (run_wizard_seestar.mjs, solve_cr2.apispec, arrow_sink.apispec, confirm_null_evidence.apispec, nova_truth, psf_physics_provider, download_gaia_chunked). Hard-stopped at time cap before completing the full WHY-stub doc sweep (⑤): verified 3 external whys + 5 doc-cited whys as a sample; the remaining ~40 stage/boundary whys went unreviewed.

cite-quality notes (all kept VERIFIED, not flipped): (a) loud_edges_c sip_convention.ts->asdf_writer.ts cite :80 lands on a doc comment describing the asdf usage, not the negation code — but toFitsSip IS called in asdf_writer.ts at :346 and :495, so the relationship is real; (b) loud_edges_c fits_writer.ts->fits_io cite :302 lands mid-sentence, the fits_writer mention is at :303 — same schema block; (c) stage_semantic_a solve->calibrate cite :1735 is a comment inside bc_measure but it does evidence calibrate reading solution.matched_stars + solution.wcs; (d) wasm_typed_array->psf_field 'AUTO-DETECT (6-vs-13)' is a psf_field.ts consumer claim asserted from the schema, not code-verified in psf_field.ts.
